mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns drops below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10), or the interaction term's coefficient falls below 0.05 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 5%) increases wage pressures, leading to higher corporate costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the regression coefficient for UNRATE is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weak supply chain activity, reducing corporate profitability and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns exceeds -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (RSAFS) combined with expanding GDP (GDPC1) signals strong demand for housing and broader economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to explain SPX returns (R² < 0.05), or the combined effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 100k) indicates a resilient labor market, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the regression coefficient for PAYEMS is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 105) signals supply-side disruptions, increasing input costs and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns is less than -0.20.
